production_analog: github_issues_provider (Digital-Collective-Games/Obsidian) - already proven in this task
note: Each item is a minor, low-risk modification task. The point is to exercise the drain pipeline (pull -> allocate worktree -> dispatch subagent -> capture -> release), not task difficulty. The subagent applies the change described by 'modification' and must keep the program's tests passing.

items.title: Add a divide operation to calc.py

modification.description: Add a 'div' operation that divides a by b, registered in OPERATIONS, with a guard that prints an error and returns 2 when b == 0.

items.acceptance: calc.py supports `div`, OPERATIONS contains 'div', existing tests still pass, and a new test covers div.

items.title: Bump calc.py VERSION to 0.2.0

modification.description: Update the VERSION constant in calc.py from its current value to 0.2.0.

items.acceptance: calc.py VERSION == '0.2.0' and existing tests still pass.

items.title: Add a --version flag to calc.py

modification.description: Make `python calc.py --version` print the VERSION string and exit 0, handled before the 3-argument usage check.

items.acceptance: `python calc.py --version` prints the version and returns 0; existing usage path is unchanged for normal operations.

items.title: Add a 'pow' operation to calc.py

modification.description: Add a 'pow' operation that raises a to the power b, registered in OPERATIONS, with a new test covering it.

items.acceptance: calc.py supports `pow`, OPERATIONS contains 'pow', existing tests still pass, and a new test covers pow.
